hints.Dominum: 'Dominum' is a noun in the accusative meaning 'the Lord'

hints.to make great: Close, but the standard gloss of 'magnificare' is 'to magnify/glorify'

hints.perfect: Perfect would be 'magnificavit'; 'magnificat' with -at ending is present

hints.facio, facere, feci, factum: These are the principal parts of 'facio' (to make/do)
hints.laudo, laudare, laudavi, laudatum: These are the principal parts of 'laudo' (to praise)
hints.amo, amare, amavi, amatum: These are the principal parts of 'amo' (to love)

hints.Magnificat: 'Magnificat' is the verb, not the subject
hints.Dominum: 'Dominum' is the direct object in the accusative

hints.ablative: Ablative would also look like 'anima' for 1st declension, but here it is the subject performing the action
hints.vocative: Vocative would be for direct address; 'anima' is the subject here

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'anima' ends in -a
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has varied nominative endings; 'anima' follows 1st declension -a pattern
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns end in -us (gen. -us); 'anima' is 1st declension

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Dominus'; 'Dominum' with -um is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'Dominus' ends in -us with -i genitive
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'Dominus' has genitive 'Domini'
hints.4th declension: 4th declension -us nouns have genitive -us; 'Dominus' has genitive 'Domini'
